title: :is()
description: Matches any element in a selector list within a single rule. It collapses repetitive selector groups and takes the specificity of its most specific argument.
html:
Post Title with a link

Normal paragraph with a regular link.

Subsection another link

More content and a paragraph link.
challenge: Paragraph links are gray, but heading links look identical to body text. Write a single rule using `.post :is(h2, h3) a` to target only heading links and turn them purple (#a855f7).
realWorldUsage: Most useful in component stylesheets with multiple element variants: :is(.card, .panel, .modal) .title targets titles in any of those containers without repeating the declaration. Also common for form normalization: :is(input, textarea, select) { border: 1px solid #ccc; } applies consistent styles across all form controls in one rule.
